conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have two children - Alex, who's 8, and Blake, who's 9. They both live with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex (8 years old) and Blake (9 years old) who both live with you.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

Are you the person responsible for Alex and Blake? By this I mean, are you the one who is mainly responsible for their day-to-day care (such as providing their meals, making sure they attend school, taking them to medical appointments, etc.)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm the one who looks after them day-to-day - I make their meals, get them ready for school, take them to doctor's appointments, all of that. They live with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming. 

Next, I need to ask: Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. 

Now I need to ask about Alex and Blake's individual circumstances:

Are both Alex and Blake under 16 years old? (I know you mentioned Alex is 8 and Blake is 9, I just need to confirm they're both under 16.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - they're both under 16. Alex is 8 and Blake is 9.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One more important question: Does anyone else also claim Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake? For example, another parent or guardian?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just clarify one final detail:

Do either Alex or Blake receive any income themselves (such as from employment or benefits in their own name), or are they in any full-time paid work or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. They're just regular school kids - they don't work or get any benefits themselves.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** Based on the information you've provided, you are **eligible for Child Benefit** for both Alex and Blake.

**For Alex (8 years old):**
You meet all the eligibility requirements - Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their day-to-day care, no one else is claiming for them, and they're not in paid work.

**For Blake (9 years old):**
You also meet all the eligibility requirements - Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their day-to-day care, no one else is claiming for them, and they're not in paid work.

You can claim Child Benefit for both children. The benefit is paid at a higher rate for the eldest child and a lower rate for any additional children.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £50,000, you may need to pay a High Income Child Benefit Charge through your tax return, though you can still claim the benefit.

Would you like any further information about making your claim?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
